message:
Fix IPC server start/stop race conditions and add verify_execute tests

Race Condition Fixes in IPCServer:
- Add startMu sync.Mutex to protect startDone channel access during concurrent
  Start() and Stop() calls.
- Add startDone chan struct{} for signaling when Start() has fully exited,
  enabling Stop() to wait for clean shutdown.
- Initialize startDone as closed channel in newIPCServer() so Stop() works
  correctly even if Start() was never called.
- In Start(): Create new startDone channel at entry, close it in defer when
  Start() exits, and set a new closed channel to signal stopped state.
- In Stop(): Wait on startDone channel before proceeding with subscriber
  cleanup, ensuring Start()'s accept loop has exited.
- Close listener in the context cancellation goroutine within Start() to
  unblock Accept() when context is cancelled.

These fixes address a subtle race where Stop() could proceed with cleanup
while Start() was still accepting connections, potentially leaving orphaned
goroutines or causing panics on subscriber map access.

New Tests for verify_execute IPC Method:
- TestIPCServer_handleVerifyExecute_VerifierNotConfigured: Verifies proper
  error response (ErrCodeInternal) when IPCServer.verifier is nil.
- TestIPCServer_handleVerifyExecute_InvalidParams: Verifies proper error
  response (ErrCodeInvalidParams) when params are not valid JSON.
- TestIPCServer_handleVerifyExecute_RequiresRequestIDAndSessionID: Verifies
  both request_id and session_id are required, with appropriate error codes.
- TestIPCServer_handleVerifyExecute_RequestNotFound: Verifies error handling
  when the referenced request does not exist in the database.

These tests ensure the execution gate's IPC endpoint correctly validates
inputs before attempting verification, maintaining the security invariant
that only fully-specified requests can be executed.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 		logger = log.Default()
 	}
 	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
+	startDone := make(chan struct{})
+	close(startDone)
 	return &IPCServer{
 		socketPath:  addr,
 		listener:    listener,
 		logger:      logger,
 		startTime:   time.Now(),
 		subscribers: make(map[int64]*subscriber),
+		startDone:   startDone,
 		ctx:         ctx,
 		cancel:      cancel,
 		cleanup:     cleanup,
@@ -99,6 +102,8 @@ type IPCServer struct {
 	ctx    context.Context
 	cancel context.CancelFunc
 	wg     sync.WaitGroup
+	startMu   sync.Mutex
+	startDone chan struct{}
 
 	// Optional verifier for execution gate checks.
 	verifier *Verifier
@@ -163,12 +168,26 @@ func NewIPCServer(socketPath string, logger *log.Logger) (*IPCServer, error) {
 
 // Start begins accepting connections. Blocks until context is cancelled.
 func (s *IPCServer) Start(ctx context.Context) error {
+	s.startMu.Lock()
+	startDone := make(chan struct{})
+	s.startDone = startDone
+	s.startMu.Unlock()
+	defer func() {
+		s.startMu.Lock()
+		close(startDone)
+		stopped := make(chan struct{})
+		close(stopped)
+		s.startDone = stopped
+		s.startMu.Unlock()
+	}()
+
 	s.logger.Info("ipc server started", "socket", s.socketPath)
 
 	// Merge with our internal context.
 	go func() {
 		<-ctx.Done()
 		s.cancel()
+		_ = s.listener.Close()
 	}()
 
 	for {
@@ -200,6 +219,11 @@ func (s *IPCServer) Stop() error {
 		s.logger.Warn("closing listener", "error", err)
 	}
 
+	s.startMu.Lock()
+	startDone := s.startDone
+	s.startMu.Unlock()
+	<-startDone
+
 	// Close all subscribers.
 	s.subscribersMu.Lock()
 	for _, sub := range s.subscribers {
